question_text: A transparent block has a critical angle of 42° for total internal reflection at its surface with air. Which statement correctly compares this block with another block that has a critical angle of 35°?

options.label: A
options.text: The block with a 42° critical angle is optically denser and has a higher refractive index than the other block.

options.label: B
options.text: The block with a 35° critical angle is optically denser and has a higher refractive index than the other block.

options.label: C
options.text: Both blocks have the same refractive index because the critical angle doesn't depend on material properties.

options.label: D
options.text: The block with a 42° critical angle allows more total internal reflection to occur because the angle is larger.

explanation: Critical angle and refractive index are related by sin(critical angle) = 1/n. A smaller critical angle means a larger refractive index (denser material). The block with 35° has sin(35°) = 0.574, giving n ≈ 1.74, while the 42° block has n ≈ 1.49. Higher refractive index means the material is optically denser.
